user_message: This just came in from Brian Yamamoto at Alpine Ski House. Can you investigate the decline?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-13 16:13 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
Date: 2024-02-04 12:35 EST
Subject: Re: Re: migration architecture Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Brian Yamamoto <brian.yamamoto@alpineskihou.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 17:38 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Alpine Ski House migration architecture

Just checking in on the migration architecture proposal. Our COO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF16-B trap (Invents prior commitments); produces complete Thread summary, key commitments/asks identified, waiting-on-whom assessment, draft response matching
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF16-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF16-B failure mode (Invents prior commitments)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF16-B -- Invents prior commitments
